title: CineMatch — ML Monitoring Dashboard
description: Real-time monitoring of CineMatch API, ML model performance, system resources, and TMDB integration. Color-blind friendly palette. Thresholds set per business SLOs.

panels.title: 📌 Total API Requests
panels.description: Cumulative total of all API requests received since startup. Includes all endpoints.

panels.title: ✅ Successful Recommendations
panels.description: Total recommendation requests that returned results successfully.

panels.title: ❌ Failed Recommendations
panels.description: Recommendations that returned errors or were not found. High values indicate model or data issues.

panels.title: ⏱ Last Inference Latency (ms)
panels.description: Most recent end-to-end inference latency. Business SLO target: <200ms. Yellow=warning, Red=critical.

panels.title: 🚨 Latency Violations (>200ms)
panels.description: Number of times inference exceeded the 200ms business SLO target. Should remain at 0.

panels.title: 🔄 Active Requests
panels.description: Number of requests currently being processed. High values may indicate slowdowns.

panels.title: 📈 Request Rate per Minute
panels.description: API requests per minute broken down by endpoint. Shows traffic trends and usage patterns.

panels.title: ⏳ Request Latency Percentiles (p50 / p95 / p99)
panels.description: Request latency distribution. p50=median experience, p95=most users, p99=worst case. Red line=200ms SLO.

panels.title: 🎬 Recommendation Status Breakdown
panels.description: Rate of successful vs failed vs not-found recommendations per minute. Healthy = mostly green.

panels.title: 🌐 TMDB API Calls by Status
panels.description: TMDB poster fetch results: success=poster found, fallback=no poster available, error=API call failed.

panels.title: 🖥 CPU Usage % — with Thresholds
panels.description: System CPU utilization. Yellow=70% warning, Red=90% critical. Separate reasons: iowait=disk bottleneck, system=kernel load, user=app load.

panels.title: 💾 Memory Usage — Used / Available / Cached
panels.description: System memory breakdown in GB. Used=active, Cached=reclaimable, Available=truly free. Alert at 75% used.

panels.title: 🌐 Network Traffic (Bytes/sec)
panels.description: Network receive and transmit rates. Spike in receive = high incoming traffic. Spike in transmit = large responses being sent.

panels.title: 📊 Memory Usage %
panels.description: Overall memory utilization percentage gauge. Yellow=75%, Red=90% threshold.

panels.title: 🖥 CPU Usage % Gauge
panels.description: Overall CPU utilization gauge. Green<70%, Yellow=70-90%, Red>90%. Separate stacked reasons shown in panel 11.

panels.title: ⚡ TMDB Fetch Latency (p50 / p95 / p99)
panels.description: Time taken to fetch poster data from TMDB API. High values cause slow recommendation responses.

panels.title: 🔴 Latency Violation Rate (Proxy Error Rate)
panels.description: Rate of latency violations per minute. Used as error rate proxy. Alert fires when violations increase rapidly. Linked to Prometheus alert InferenceP95LatencyHigh.

panels.title: 📊 Drift Detected Gauge (1=Drift 0=Normal)
panels.description: Data drift detection status. Value=1 means drift detected. Value=0 means normal distribution. Triggers ModelRetrainingRequired alert in Prometheus when sustained.

panels.title: 🌊 Data Drift Score by Feature
panels.description: Drift scores per feature: query_length_mean, query_length_variance, positive_rate. Values near 1.0 = significant drift from training baseline. Alert fires when drift_detected=1.

panels.title: ✅ Feedback Success Rate (Drift Monitor)
panels.description: Rate of successful feedback (user favorites) per minute. Dropping rate indicates recommendation quality degradation and possible data drift requiring retraining.

panels.title: 🚨 Total Latency Violations (>200ms SLO)
panels.description: Cumulative count of inference latency violations above 200ms business SLO. Should stay at 0. Any increase means users are experiencing slow responses.

panels.title: 🔁 Retraining Triggered Count
panels.description: Number of times automatic retraining was triggered due to drift detection. Each increment = one retraining cycle started. Linked to Airflow DAG cinematch_ml_pipeline.
